name: Moralzarzal, Manzanares y Mataelpino — Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Mountain biking around Moralzarzal offers a diverse natural landscape situated in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The region features extensive trail networks, characterized by granite formations and abundant water sources. Pine and oak forests are common, providing varied terrain for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Moralzarzal The most popular mountain bike trail is Moralzarzal, Manzanares y Mataelpino — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route explores the diverse terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is ADESGAM Route: Moralzarzal - Santillana Reservoir Loop, a moderate 25.5 miles (41.1 km) path. This route offers views of the Santillana Reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Santillana Reservoir Bridge – Santillana Reservoir loop from Moralzarzal, a 18.9 miles (30.5 km) trail leading through the Santillana Reservoir area, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Moralzarzal is defined by Sierra de Guadarrama foothills, granite formations, and pine and oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Moralzarzal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Moralzarzal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Moralzarzal?
A: Moralzarzal offers a substantial network of mountain bike trails, with over 28 routes documented on komoot. The region is particularly rich, boasting 91 designated mountain biking trails in total, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for all skill levels in Moralzarzal?
A: Yes, Moralzarzal caters to all skill levels. You'll find a good distribution of routes, with 8 easy, 10 moderate, and 10 difficult trails available on komoot. The diverse terrain, from gentle paths to challenging descents like 'Trail To Hell' and 'Toboganes', ensures there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Moralzarzal?
A: The terrain around Moralzarzal is highly varied, situated in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. You'll encounter granite formations, abundant water sources, and ride through pine and oak forests. The landscape also features significant elevation changes, offering both climbs and descents.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Moralzarzal?
A: The trails offer stunning views of peaks like El Yelmo, La Maliciosa, and Siete Picos. You can also encounter natural features such as the Navacerrada River, various springs, and streams. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Moralzarzal?
A: Yes, many routes in Moralzarzal are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961438981/adesgam-route-moralzarzal-santillana-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;ADESGAM Route: Moralzarzal - Santillana Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.5-mile path offering scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19219375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir Bridge – Santillana Reservoir loop from Moralzarzal&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.9 miles long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Moralzarzal?
A: Moralzarzal experiences a mountain climate with cold winters and dry, hot summers. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters may bring colder conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Moralzarzal?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations vary, the presence of 8 easy trails suggests options suitable for less experienced riders or families. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and less technical terrain, allowing for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Moralzarzal?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama region, where Moralzarzal is located, is generally dog-friendly, but it's always best to check specific trail regulations or park rules. Many trails pass through natural areas where dogs are welcome, often requiring them to be on a leash to protect wildlife.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems around Moralzarzal are home to various wildlife. You might spot small insectivorous birds, owls, jays, and woodpeckers. Birds of prey like goshawks, eagles, and falcons also inhabit the area. Keep an eye out for reptiles and amphibians, especially near water sources.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, beyond the trails, you can explore historical sites. The 16th-17th century San Miguel Arcángel Church and a 19th-century fountain are in Moralzarzal town square. Nearby, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an Isabelline Gothic fortress, is also worth a visit.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Moralzarzal?
A: The mountain bike trails in Moralzarzal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the extensive network that offers options for all abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, trails like 'Trail To Hell' are known for their length and significant elevation descent. The Cerro del Telégrafo area is particularly popular for enduro mountain biking, featuring demanding descents and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5319893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Cross – Moralzarzal Town Hall loop from Moralzarzal&lt;/a&gt; is also rated as difficult.

Enjoy the views, the mountains and the Castle of Manzanares in the background, the Santillana reservoir at your feet.
The route between the reservoir and the Batán bridge is recommended

The Plaza del Pueblo in Manzanares el Real is the heart of this charming town in the Community of Madrid. This square is a meeting and celebration place for residents and visitors alike, and is surrounded by historic and picturesque buildings.

On the right bank of the Manzanares River, the Old Castle of Manzanares El Real stands on a hill, guarding La Pedriza, the Cañada Real Segoviana and the river itself. The fortress, whose shape and exterior walls are preserved, was the first castle inhabited by the Mendoza family and which served as a defense against Moors and Christians.

From here you can see the entire Sierra de los Porrones and the depression where the Santillana reservoir is located. You can also see the typical agricultural landscape of the Sierra de Guadarrama with its pastures and cattle on the farms.

The Hermitage of San Isidro is located on the slopes of El Boalo, very close to the town centre. It was built with local masonry by the local residents, reflecting the devotion of the inhabitants of El Boalo to San Isidro, patron saint of cattle breeders and farmers.

Every 15th of May, a pilgrimage is held in honour of San Isidro, where locals and visitors take part in a day in the countryside full of hospitality and festivities. The hermitage is located at the foot of Peña Mediodía and next to the ecological recreational area of the same name.

Section of gravel and sand track ideal for pedaling quietly and enjoying the classic country landscape of the Sierra de Guadarrama. To the north you can see the peak of La Maliciosa and the ball of the world and to the east La Pedriza.
